question: Big Bash League: Adelaide Strikers vs Melbourne Renegades (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Adelaide Strikers and Melbourne Renegades scheduled for January 17 2026 in Big Bash League.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: Big Bash League: Adelaide Strikers vs Melbourne Renegades (Game 1) - Who wins the toss?
description:
This market refers to the pre-match coin toss for the cricket match between Adelaide Strikers and Melbourne Renegades scheduled for 2026-01-17 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the official coin toss result (the team recorded as having won the toss) as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.

description:

question: Big Bash League: Adelaide Strikers vs Melbourne Renegades (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Adelaide Strikers Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Adelaide Strikers and Melbourne Renegades scheduled for 2026-01-17 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Adelaide Strikers will be considered correct if  Adelaide Strikers is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Renegades will be considered correct if Melbourne Renegades is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

This market will resolve to "Quinnipiac Bobcats" if the Quinnipiac Bobcats win the game by 5 or more points.

question: Big Bash League: Perth Scorchers vs Melbourne Stars (Game 1) - Most Sixes Perth Scorchers Winner
description:
This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Perth Scorchers and Melbourne Stars scheduled for 2026-01-17 in Big Bash League.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Perth Scorchers will be considered correct if Perth Scorchers is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Melbourne Stars.The outcome corresponding to Melbourne Stars will be considered correct if Melbourne Stars is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Perth Scorchers.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Joburg Super Kings vs Pretoria Capitals (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Joburg Super Kings and Pretoria Capitals scheduled for January 17 2026 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Joburg Super Kings vs Pretoria Capitals (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Joburg Super Kings Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Joburg Super Kings and Pretoria Capitals scheduled for 2026-01-17 in SA20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if  Joburg Super Kings is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

This market refers to which team hits the greater number of sixes in the cricket match between Joburg Super Kings and Pretoria Capitals scheduled for 2026-01-17 in SA20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if Joburg Super Kings is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Pretoria Capitals.The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if Pretoria Capitals is officially recorded as hitting more sixes than Joburg Super Kings.
If both teams record the same number of sixes, the market will resolve to "Draw".
The market will resolve according to the team that hits the greater number of sixes in the match regardless of if the match is not competed (e.g. Due to weather conditions)/
Only sixes scored from the bat (off any delivery- legal or not) will count towards the total sixes. Overthrows and extras do not count. Sixes scored in a super over do not count.
In First Class games, only first innings sixes will count.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without official sixes statistics being recorded, the market will resolve "Draw".
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and official statistics are available.

question: SA20: Joburg Super Kings vs Pretoria Capitals (Game 1) - Team Top Batter Joburg Super Kings Winner
description:
This market refers to which team's player records the highest individual run total in the cricket match between Joburg Super Kings and Pretoria Capitals scheduled for 2026-01-17 in SA20.
This market resolves according to the finalized match statistics as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Joburg Super Kings will be considered correct if the highest individual score in the match is recorded exclusively by a player from Joburg Super Kings.
The outcome corresponding to Pretoria Capitals will be considered correct if the highest individual score is recorded exclusively by a player from Pretoria Capitals.

question: SA20: Durban's Super Giants vs Paarl Royals (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Durban's Super Giants and Paarl Royals scheduled for January 17 2026 in SA20.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the winner determined by that tiebreak will be used for resolution. If the match ends tied and no on-field tiebreak is used or available under the playing conditions (e.g., group-stage ODI with no Super Over), the market will resolve 50-50.

If the match is postponed/rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed. If the match is permanently canceled or abandoned or otherwise is completed without a winner, the market resolves 50-50

question: SA20: Durban's Super Giants vs Paarl Royals (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Durban's Super Giants Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Durban's Super Giants and Paarl Royals scheduled for 2026-01-17 in SA20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Durban's Super Giants will be considered correct if  Durban's Super Giants is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Paarl Royals will be considered correct if Paarl Royals is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

question: BPL: Durdanto Dhaka vs Chattogram Challengers (Game 1)
description:

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: BPL: Noakhali vs Rangpur Riders (Game 1)
description:
This market refers to the cricket match between Noakhali and Rangpur Riders scheduled for January 17 2026 in BPL.

This market resolves according to the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.  

DLS/DRS, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a winner are treated as ordinary wins.

question: BPL: Noakhali vs Rangpur Riders (Game 1) - Toss Match Double Noakhali Winner
description:
This market refers to the combination of the pre-match coin toss and the final match result for the cricket match between Noakhali and Rangpur Riders scheduled for 2026-01-17 in BPL T20.
This market resolves according to (1) the official toss result and (2) the finalized match result as published by https://www.espncricinfo.com/.
The outcome corresponding to Noakhali will be considered correct if  Noakhali is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
The outcome corresponding to Rangpur Riders will be considered correct if Rangpur Riders is officially recorded as winning both the toss and the match.
In all other cases - including where the toss and match winners differ, or where no match winner is recorded - the market will resolve to Neither.
DLS/DRS adjustments, over-rate penalties, forfeit/walkover, Super Over, or any other on-field ruling that leads the competition to declare a match winner are treated as ordinary wins. If the match ends tied and the playing conditions provide an on-field tiebreak (e.g., Super Over), the tiebreak winner will be considered the match winner for this market. If the match ends tied with no tiebreak used or available, no team will be considered to have won the match and the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is permanently canceled, abandoned, or otherwise completed without an official match winner being declared, the market will resolve to Neither.
If the match is postponed or rescheduled, the market remains open until the listed fixture is completed and will then resolve as described above.

In the first half of the NBA game between Knicks and Trail Blazers, scheduled for January 11 at 6:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if the Knicks and Trail Blazers combine to score 116 or more points in the first half.

If the combined first half total is less than 116, this market will resolve to "Under".

The result will be determined based on the score at halftime only.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

question: US strikes Iran by January 12, 2026?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the US initiates a drone, missile, or air strike on Iranian soil or any official Iranian embassy or consulate between the time of this market's creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

For the purposes of this market, a qualifying "strike" is defined as the use of aerial bombs, drones or missiles (including cruise or ballistic missiles) launched by US military forces that impact Iranian ground territory or any official Iranian embassy or consulate (e.g., if a weapons depot on Iranian soil is hit by an US missile, this market will resolve to "Yes").

Missiles or drones which are intercepted and surface-to-air missile strikes will not be sufficient for a "Yes" resolution regardless of whether they land on Iranian territory or cause damage.

Actions such as artillery fire, small arms fire, FPV or ATGM strikes directly, ground incursions, naval shelling, cyberattacks, or other operations conducted by US ground operatives will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

question: Fearnley vs. Vukic: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Jacob Fearnley and Aleksandar Vukic in the Adelaide International, Qualification, scheduled for January 11 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
groupItemTitle: Adelaide International, Qualification: Jacob Fearnley vs Aleksandar Vukic Set 1 O/U 8.5

events.title: Adelaide International, Qualification: Jacob Fearnley vs Aleksandar Vukic
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Jacob Fearnley and Aleksandar Vukic in the Adelaide International, Qualification, scheduled for January 11 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Fearnley' if Jacob Fearnley advances against Aleksandar Vukic.

This market will resolve to 'Vukic' if Aleksandar Vukic advances against Jacob Fearnley.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Spread: Olympique de Marseille (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Ligue 1 game, scheduled for January 24 at 3:05 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Olympique de Marseille" if Olympique de Marseille win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Racing Club de Lens".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligue1.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

question: Misolic vs. Shevchenko: Set 1 Games O/U 8.5
description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Filip Misolic and Alexander Shevchenko in the Adelaide International, Qualification, scheduled for January 11 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed in the first set equals or exceeds 9. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."
Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
If the first set is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. If the match is canceled before play begins, or delayed beyond 7 days without commencement, this market will also resolve 50-50.
Resolution will be based on official ATP results.
groupItemTitle: Adelaide International, Qualification: Filip Misolic vs Alexander Shevchenko Set 1 O/U 8.5

events.title: Adelaide International, Qualification: Filip Misolic vs Alexander Shevchenko
events.description:
This market refers on the tennis match between Filip Misolic and Alexander Shevchenko in the Adelaide International, Qualification, scheduled for January 11 at 6:30PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Misolic' if Filip Misolic advances against Alexander Shevchenko.

This market will resolve to 'Shevchenko' if Alexander Shevchenko advances against Filip Misolic.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

question: Ethereum Up or Down - January 12, 2:00AM-2:15AM ET
description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Ethereum price at the end of the time range specified in the title is greater than or equal to the price at the beginning of that range. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Down".
The resolution source for this market is information from Chainlink, specifically the ETH/USD data stream available at https://data.chain.link/streams/eth-usd.
Please note that this market is about the price according to Chainlink data stream ETH/USD, not according to other sources or spot markets.
